mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the fed funds rate declines over any 60-day period but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals deteriorating real estate demand and broader economic weakness, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST is below 1300 but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and declining retail sales indicate a weakening consumer sector that erodes corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as the labor market deteriorates and spending contracts.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling RSAFS has a Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is strong alongside elevated inflation, the economy risks overheating and monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns. The mechanism is conditional on an inflation regime above 3.5% year-over-year.
failure_condition: If when CPIAUCSL Y/Y exceeds 3.5% and INDPRO is above its 12-month trend, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently rising payroll employment indicates a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as bullish sentiment persists.
failure_condition: If the 12-month change in PAYEMS is positive but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5% signals a material deterioration in labor market conditions that historically precedes economic contractions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession fears intensify.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in housing starts and industrial production signal a synchronized downturn in demand and supply that deepens recession risk, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as economic momentum falters.
failure_condition: If simultaneous month-over-month declines in both HOUST and INDPRO are not followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth has a positive effect on equity returns only when the federal funds rate is low enough to support borrowing and consumption. In a low-rate regime, rising retail sales reflect genuine consumer strength and propel stock prices higher, while in high-rate regimes the effect is muted or negative.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS is below 3%, the correlation between RSAFS growth and S&P 500 forward 20-day returns has a Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.
